data.title: Bitcoin's profit cycle dips below $90,000 for the first time since 2023, indicating negative trends in on-chain realized profits.
data.description: The drop in Bitcoin's value to below $90,000 has caused on-chain realized profit metrics to enter negative territory, marking a level not seen since the previous bear market.

data.title: Registration open for Clarion Safety Systems' free 2026 webinar on maximizing on-product warnings. MILFOR, Pa., Jan. 23 /PRNewswire
data.description: A virtual event will delve into symbol-based warnings, standards unification, and the significance of warnings in contemporary equipment design. Clarion Safety Systems revealed that registration is available for its upcoming no-cost live webinar. The event will discuss how to optimize safety and promote consistency in visual warnings. Scheduled for January 23, 2026, the webinar aims to educate attendees on the crucial role of warnings in enhancing safety standards, with insights from industry experts. Don't miss this opportunity to gain valuable knowledge.

data.title: Secretary Wright approves backup generation for data centers ahead of Winter Storm Fern. Compass Datacenters in support.
data.description: In Dallas on January 22, 2026, Compass Datacenters expressed full support for Secretary Wright's authorization permitting data centers to employ backup power generation in preparation for an unprecedented winter storm projected to affect a significant portion of the nation. This decision grants Compass the ability to ensure operational continuity and safeguard essential data infrastructure amidst the challenging weather conditions.

data.title: US prosecutors drop case against former OpenSea manager Nathaniel Chastain, who appealed wire fraud and money laundering conviction.
data.description: A former OpenSea manager, Nathaniel Chastain, has had the wire fraud and money laundering conviction dropped by the Justice Department. Chastain appealed the case and was successful in having it overturned.

data.title: Amazon plans to lay off corporate workers as part of an overhaul, aiming to cut around 30,000 roles.
data.description: Amazon.com Inc., a major company listed on NASDAQ under the symbol AMZN, is reportedly gearing up for another round of layoffs affecting its white-collar corporate employees. The Seattle-headquartered company is aiming to simplify its operations and boost efficiencies by reducing its workforce. According to two individuals familiar with the situation cited in a Reuters report, Amazon is slated to commence the layoffs from Tuesday onwards. The move is part of a larger plan to eliminate approximately 30,000 corporate positions as the company looks to streamline its operations following the rapid expansion and hiring surge prompted by increased demand during the COVID-19 pandemic. In October 2025, Amazon had already slashed around 14,000 corporate jobs, and a similar number of positions are expected to be cut in the upcoming round of layoffs.

data.title: DOGE price could hit $0.16-$0.175 by Jan. 2026. Analysts suggest despite $0.125 consolidation, neutral RSI & $0.13 resistance.
data.description: Analysts are optimistic about the future of Dogecoin (DOGE), predicting a potential increase to a range of $0.16 to $0.175 by the end of the month. Currently, DOGE is experiencing consolidation at $0.125. Technical analysis reveals a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) and significant resistance at the $0.13 mark. This projection suggests a potential upward trend in the price of DOGE in the near future.

data.title: $48 million in seized Bitcoin lost to phishing scam in South Korea; discovered during routine inspection of assets.
data.description: During a routine inspection of seized financial assets in South Korea, prosecutors found that a certain amount of Bitcoin was missing from the collection.

data.title: Kerry Washington receives Elevate Prize Catalyst Award at Sundance, joins docuseries. Elevate Prize aims to tell important stories with courage.
data.description: Elevate has joined forces with an Emmy award-winning actor and activist to spotlight important stories amidst challenges to democracy, civil rights, and creativity. The Elevate Prize Foundation recently unveiled this partnership in Park City, Utah on January 22, 2026, emphasizing the importance of courage in storytelling.

data.title: Polygon (MATIC) aims for $0.45-$0.52 rebound by March '26 from oversold $0.38 support, per analysts.
data.description: The digital asset Polygon (MATIC) is currently experiencing oversold conditions as it hovers around the $0.38 support level. Analysts are anticipating a potential recovery in the range of $0.45 to $0.52 within the next 4 to 6 weeks, despite ongoing bearish signals in the market.
